title: Zakaria and the River That Argued
logline: Zakaria fights Salma until they discover they were always the same water.
story: Certain they were opposites and enemies, Zakaria, who carried remembering what was forgotten, ran headlong against Salma, who was quiet harp music. Each tried to push the other back uphill, and they struggled until both were spent and still. In that stillness Zakaria noticed they had pooled together and could no longer tell which water had been which. What Zakaria had fought so hard in Salma was only the part of Zakaria that had never been lived. Joined, they became a river wide enough to carry them both.
moral: What you fight hardest in another is most often the unlived part of yourself.
attribution: a Pagarek parable, from Project Genesis
